creator: markj@FreeBSD.org
text:
I used linprocfs because that's what security/sssd/files/patch-src__util__find_uid.c uses.  I'm not sure if security/sssd is still used much, though.  Really the native FreeBSD way to do this is using the sysctl interface, like `procstat -a credentials` would do.

Using FreeBSD procfs seems fine but the patch doesn't look right.  The 13th field is the group list, but this code is supposed to be looking for the real UID of the running process, which is the 11th field.
